ref_text_raw: For example, each year students from Bennet School in North Carolina design a website about their trip to the State Capital, each year the website gets remodeled, but old versions are kept online to serve as a scrapbook.
ref_text_normalized: for example each year students from bennet school in north carolina design a website about their trip to the state capital each year the website gets remodeled but old versions are kept online to serve as a scrapbook
text: For example, each year students from Bennett School in North Carolina design a website about their trip to the state capital. Each year the website gets remodeled, but older versions are kept online to serve as a scrapbook.

params.note: autoDecodingConfig; punctuation etc. = API defaults (R-2.4); no diarization

metadata.prompt:
Transcribe the following speech segment in English. Follow these specific instructions for formatting the answer:
* Only output the transcript, with no newlines.
* When transcribing numbers, write the digits, i.e. write 1.7 and not one point seven, and write 100 instead of one hundred etc.
* Only output the transcript, exactly as said in the speech segment.
* Do not continue the speech segment.
* Include everything said in the speech segment.
* Use mathematical notation where needed, eg. x ^ 6Y.
* If there is background speech that is not part of the main conversation, discard the background speech and only output the main conversation.
* If there is no clear speech, just output [BACKGROUND].
* Respond in the original language of the speech, English.

alternatives.transcript: For example, each year students from Bennett School in North Carolina design a website about their trip to the state capital. Each year the website gets remodeled, but older versions are kept online to serve as a scrapbook.
